title: Monet Tracker rename, chart color update, and UI polish

requirements.summary: Update the Dashboard weekly 'Daily Spending' bar chart to use a different existing chart color token for clearer visual distinction.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/components/charts/WeeklySpendBarChart.tsx`, the `<Bar ... />` fill no longer uses `hsl(var(--chart-1))` and instead uses a different existing chart token (e.g., `hsl(var(--chart-2))`).
The weekly bar chart remains readable in both light and dark themes (bars, axes, tooltip).
No other charts change color unless necessary to preserve visual contrast.

file_operations.description: Change the Recharts `<Bar>` fill from `hsl(var(--chart-1))` to a different existing chart token (e.g., `hsl(var(--chart-2))`) and confirm tooltip/axis styling remains readable in light/dark mode.

requirements.summary: Rename the app to Monet Tracker across all user-facing surfaces (shell, settings/about, and document title).
acceptanceCriteria: Header brand text in `frontend/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` displays "Monet Tracker" instead of "ExpenseTracker".
Footer copyright line in `frontend/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx` references "Monet Tracker".
The Settings "About" description in `frontend/src/screens/SettingsScreen.tsx` references "Monet Tracker" (and no longer mentions "ExpenseTracker").
All user-facing occurrences of the old app name are removed from the frontend.

file_operations.description: Update header brand text and footer copyright copy to use “Monet Tracker”, ensuring no remaining user-facing “ExpenseTracker” references in this shell.
Update the About section copy to reference “Monet Tracker” and remove any mention of the previous app name.
Update the HTML document `<title>` to include “Monet Tracker” and remove the old app naming.

requirements.summary: Polish UI styling for a more professional, cohesive finance-tracker aesthetic across the app shell and primary screens without adding features.
acceptanceCriteria: The AppShell header (brand + nav) has improved visual polish (consistent alignment, spacing, and typographic hierarchy) while keeping existing navigation items and routes unchanged.
Cards and key sections on primary screens (Dashboard, Add Expense, Expense List, Settings) maintain consistent spacing and visual rhythm (e.g., section headers, card padding, grid gaps).
UI remains accessible: text contrast stays acceptable in both light and dark mode, and interactive elements retain clear hover/focus states.
No backend changes are introduced as part of this polish task.

file_operations.description: Refine header/footer/main layout styling for improved hierarchy and spacing (e.g., brand typography, header spacing, footer density) while keeping existing routes and navigation intact. Use existing shadcn-ui composition patterns already present; do not modify any files under `frontend/src/components/ui`. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Polish top navigation presentation (button spacing, alignment, responsive label behavior, and active/hover/focus clarity) using existing shadcn-ui `Button` without changing nav items/routes. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Normalize screen-level spacing and typography (page header spacing, Tabs spacing, grid gaps) to match a cohesive rhythm; keep information and layout structure the same. Use existing shadcn-ui components as composed today. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Align page header spacing/typographic hierarchy and card section spacing with the rest of the app for a more polished finance-tracker look, without changing form behavior. Use existing shadcn-ui Card composition; do not edit `frontend/src/components/ui`. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Improve consistency of section headers, card padding, and vertical rhythm (filters card + list area), keeping existing functionality unchanged. Use existing shadcn-ui components as-is. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Polish settings page typography and spacing (header, card spacing, descriptions) for a more cohesive look while preserving accessibility and current interactions. Use existing shadcn-ui components as composed. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Make small global style refinements that improve typography hierarchy and subtle surface feel (e.g., heading tracking/weights already defined, focus ring utility usage consistency), ensuring contrast remains acceptable in light/dark mode and without introducing new features or modifying shadcn-ui component source.
